title: Left-comb Laplacian words
summary: Defines rank-general nested Laplacian monomials on a left-comb binary tree, their degree indices, vertex updates, multinomial coefficients, and recursive evaluation.

mathematicalContent.heading: Tree index
mathematicalContent.body: A full binary tree with r ordered leaves has 2r−1 vertices. A comb index assigns a nonnegative Laplacian exponent to every vertex and requires their sum to be k.

mathematicalContent.heading: Left-comb evaluation
mathematicalContent.body: The recursion repeatedly multiplies the previously formed left subtree by the last differentiated input, then applies the Laplacian power stored at the new internal vertex.

mathematicalContent.heading: Combinatorial operations
mathematicalContent.body: The file can increment one vertex exponent and attaches the multinomial coefficient k!/∏ᵥαᵥ! to an index.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the number of vertices used by the left-comb encoding of a full binary tree with r leaves.
Stores one natural Laplacian exponent at each left-comb vertex, with total degree k.
Introduces PolyTreeIndex as an alias for the current left-comb index type.

correspondence.leanType: def totalPower {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) : ℕ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the total Laplacian degree by summing all vertex exponents.

correspondence.leanType: theorem totalPower_eq {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) : I.totalPower = k
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that the total degree of an index of type CombIndex(r,k) is k.

correspondence.leanType: def powerAt {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (i : ℕ) : ℕ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Reads the exponent at a natural-number vertex code and returns zero outside the valid range.

correspondence.leanType: theorem powerAt_of_fin {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : I.powerAt v.val = I.power v
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Shows that natural-number lookup agrees with the stored exponent for every valid finite vertex.

correspondence.leanType: def addAt {r s : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r s) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : CombIndex r (s + 1)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Adds one unit of Laplacian degree at a selected vertex.

correspondence.leanType: theorem addAt_power_same {r s : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r s) (v : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)) : (I.addAt v).power v = I.power v + 1
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At the selected vertex, addAt increases the exponent by exactly one.

correspondence.leanType: theorem addAt_power_ne {r s : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r s) {v x : Fin (leftCombVertexCount r)} (hxv : x ≠ v) : (I.addAt v).power x = I.power x
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At every other vertex, addAt leaves the exponent unchanged.

correspondence.leanType: def multinomialCoeffQ {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) : ℚ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the multinomial coefficient attached to the vertex exponents.

correspondence.leanType: def leftCombEvalFrom (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : (r : ℕ) → (ℕ → ℕ) → CalConf.MultiInput r → Function Conf
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Recursively evaluates the nested left-comb Laplacian word from a raw exponent function.

correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator r
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Turns a degree-k comb index into the corresponding rank-r ambient multi-operator.

correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply {r k : ℕ} (I : CombIndex r k) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : I.toOperator CalConf F = leftCombEvalFrom CalConf r I.powerAt F
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds the operator associated with a comb index into the recursive evaluator.

references.locator: Main ambient construction and tangentiality results
references.note: Motivation for rank-general nested Laplacian words; the Lean tree complex goes beyond what is currently instantiated.

limitations: This is a syntax and bookkeeping layer. It fixes only the left-comb tree, defines rank zero to evaluate as zero, and proves no homogeneity, tangentiality, commutator, symmetry, or equivalence with arbitrary rooted binary trees.
